DISCOVERY 302, RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. THE COURT CONTINUES HEARING ON PLAINTIFF ALLISON JOHNSON, BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO TO SIX FORM INTERROGATORIES AND ALL REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS TO MAY 24, 2013 AT 9:00 AM. JUDGE PRO TEM DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SAN MATEO COUNTY IS GRANTED. SAN MATEO HAS THE ABSOLUTE RIGHT TO REMOVE THIS ACTION TO ITS HOME COUNTY. (CCP 394.) IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE INJURY OCCURRED THERE. SAN MATEO FILED THE MOTION WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME. (SEE ADAMS V. SUPERIOR COURT, 226 CAL. APP. 2D 365, 367 (1964).) SAN MATEO ACTED SENSIBLY BY FILING THIS MOTION WITH ITS ANSWER AFTER THE CASE WAS AT ISSUE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. SAN MATEO COUNTY IMPERMISSIBLY RELIES ON EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE THAT IS NOT JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE TO SUPPORT ITS ARGUMENT THAT ONLY CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO CAN BE LIABLE BECAUSE ONLY IT ALLEGEDLY HELD RESPONSIBILITY FOR PLAINTIFF'S CASE PLAN. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-27-2013 Continued To Sep-11-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue Or In The Alternative
Off Calendar Jun-26-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JUDITH A. HOLIBER, TEL. NO. (650) 363-4747 EXT. 4747 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
KAREN KIRBY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3891 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CCSF.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO's Motion To Transfer Venue to San Mateo County is granted. San Mateo has the absolute right to remove this action to its home county. (CCP 394.) It is undisputed that the injury occurred there. San Mateo filed the motion within a reasonable time. (See Adams v. Superior Court, 226 Cal. App. 2d 365, 367 (1964).) San Mateo acted sensibly by filing this motion with its answer after the case was at issue. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: alexis bastedo, esq., 760.697.1330, for plaintiff allison johnson.
daniel alweiss, deputy attorney general, 415.703.1276, for defendant state of california by and through california department of social services.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the second - fourth causes of action only. (CCP 430.10(e).) State of California is immune from liability under the licensing immunity per Gov. Code 818.4 for the alleged violations of Health & Safety Code 1522.1, 1534, 1538. Not every instance of the term "shall" implicates a mandatory duty that is enforceable by a private plaintiff. (MacDonald v. St. of Calif. (1991) 230 Cal.App.3d 319, 330.) Unlike Corona v. State of California, the Department here exercises its discretion in determining whether to issue, revoke or deny a license under Health & Safety Code 1522.1, 1534, 1538. (Corona v. State (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 723, 728 - 729, 732.) To the extent that Plaintiff requests leave to amend to add additional causes of action against the county defendants, the request made in opposition to State of California's demurrer is insufficient. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: alexis bastedo, esq., 760.697.1330, for plaintiff allison johnson; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. San Mateo County impermissibly relies on extrinsic evidence that is not judicially noticeable to support its argument that only City and County of San Francisco can be liable because only it allegedly held responsibility for Plaintiff's case plan. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
